negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing Scheduler state cleanup semantics that remove calls and auxiliary indexes only for terminal/explicit eviction events and retain retryable failures/skips.
related_but_insufficient_code: Current scheduled verification reads Scheduler parent storage; there is no in-memory scheduler state cleanup/event classifier.

rationale: The packet repeatedly requires scheduler call cleanup to remove call records and auxiliary indexes. A newer bounded neighbor narrows the terminal set so execution failures and skip events retain calls for retry, superseding the earlier removal of insufficient-funds and TTL-expired skips.
statement: Scheduler state cleanup must remove a call and all auxiliary indexes for cancellation, completion, expiration, and explicit eviction, while retaining calls for execution failures and retryable skip events so they can be selected again.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate scheduler state with a call in calls, execution_counts, origin_tx_hashes, and frequency_index, process Cancel, Complete, and Expire events, and assert all stores remove the call.
Process ExecutionFailed, insufficient-funds skip, and TTL-expired skip events for tracked calls and assert the calls remain eligible for retry according to the current event classifier.
Call SynchronousScheduler::evict_call for an existing and a missing call id and assert the existing call is removed while the missing id is a no-op.
summary: Scheduler state terminal-only cleanup behavior is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Process terminal and non-terminal Scheduler events against scheduler state; current port has no event-state processor.
title: Scheduler state removes only terminal or explicitly evicted calls
